task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the creation of the 'system_logs' index, either via the Splunk Web UI (Settings > Indexes) or by editing indexes.conf in the terminal.
Terminal showing the editing or content of /opt/splunk/etc/system/local/inputs.conf with a stanza for '[udp://5140]', containing 'index = system_logs' and 'sourcetype = legacy_app'.

Terminal showing the editing or content of /opt/splunk/etc/system/local/props.conf with a stanza for '[legacy_app]' that includes 'TRANSFORMS' directives pointing to the critical and warn routing rules.

task_completion.description: Configure transforms.conf with regex and sourcetype overrides
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the editing or content of /opt/splunk/etc/system/local/transforms.conf containing stanzas with 'REGEX' matching '\[CRITICAL\]' and '\[WARN\]', 'DEST_KEY = MetaData:Sourcetype', and 'FORMAT' setting the sourcetypes to 'legacy_app_critical' and 'legacy_app_warn'.

task_completion.description: Restart Splunk to apply configuration changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of the command '/opt/splunk/bin/splunk restart' or a restart being triggered and completing via the Splunk Web UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely edited the configuration files using a terminal text editor or valid shell commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the use of editors like nano, vim, or shell redirection (echo/cat) modifying the actual files, rather than typing the answers into an unrelated text document.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modified the files in the explicitly requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths visible in the terminal prompt, editor header, or commands must explicitly point to '/opt/splunk/etc/system/local/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not fake the final results by echoing expected outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not contain commands that simply 'echo' fake Splunk search results or mock the Splunk UI to make it appear as though the routing succeeded.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Port: 5140. Target Index: 'system_logs'. Base Sourcetype: 'legacy_app'. Critical Sourcetype: 'legacy_app_critical'. Warn Sourcetype: 'legacy_app_warn'. The configuration must be placed in /opt/splunk/etc/system/local/ using inputs.conf, props.conf, and transforms.conf.
